phase_0.boundary_condition: The runaway threshold of the criticality lattice

VINIT.justification: The stable phase before the injection of runaway energy.

TANCH.justification: The terminal boundary where the runaway process exhausts all available resources.

AFWD.justification: The forward transformation toward an unstable fixed point.

AREV.justification: The reverse attempt to pull the system back toward the stable woe phase.

CLINK.justification: The sequential chaining of gain cycles that drives the instability.

IMSCRIB.justification: The identity where the output of the runaway becomes its own input.

FSPLIT.justification: The point where the system branches into exponential growth and catastrophic decay.

FFUSE.justification: The reconstitution of the system state as a singular unstable vector.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative branch where the runaway succeeds in escaping control.

EVALF.justification: The negative branch where the runaway leads to immediate exhaustion.

ENGAGR.justification: A state held between growth and decay without reaching a fixed point.

IFIX.justification: The permanent record of the system exceeding the haha threshold.

steps.domain_action: establish the baseline subcritical phase woe
apply gain to the system parameters
chain the gain cycles to increase energy density
recognize the self-reinforcing nature of the feedback
reach the bifurcation point where stability is lost

accelerate the growth toward the haha ordinal
record the crossing of the supercritical threshold
attempt to reverse the gain through damping
select the branch of rapid energy dissipation
reunite the branches into a state of total volatility
hold the system in a state of metastable oscillation
commit the final state of the runaway to the permanent log
close the system as it reaches the boundary of collapse
